title: Surveying with EM38 + Dual EMS
subtitle: Electromagnetic induction soil survey — field SOP
author: Precision Ag
owner: Document owner: Brandon Miller, Digital Analyst

chapters.label: Introduction
chapters.tag: What this survey is, when to use it, how to read this manual
chapters.overview: This manual is the field reference for running an EM38 + Dual EMS electromagnetic induction survey end to end — from loading the rig to handing off clean, exported data. Use it on the job: every chapter opens with a quick overview, then the detailed steps.
chapters.at_a_glance: EM induction measures apparent soil electrical conductivity (ECa), which we use as a proxy for texture, moisture and salinity variation across a paddock. Higher quality in = higher quality maps out.

items: Tabs run down the left edge — tap any chapter to jump straight to it.
HOME (top-left) returns to the cover; CONTENTS (top-right) returns to the chapter index.
Every chapter starts with an overview so you can orient before diving in.
Use your PDF reader's search (magnifying glass) to find any term, part number or error message.

blocks.title: FIRST TIME?
blocks.text: Read Introduction → Equipment Needed → the setup chapter for your vehicle in full before your first solo survey. After that, the Equipment Needed checklist and Troubleshooting table are the pages you'll actually live in.

items: Job confirmed in [CONFIRM: Salesforce / scheduling system] and paddock boundary loaded
Weather and ground conditions suitable (see Survey Setup)
Vehicle for this job confirmed (Polaris or Ute)
All gear from the Equipment Needed checklist packed and powered
Landholder access / gate / biosecurity requirements noted

blocks.title: VERSION CHECK
blocks.text: Field procedures change as firmware and software update. Check the footer of this page — if the version on your device is older than the one published on [CONFIRM: SharePoint / docs site], grab the latest before heading out.

chapters.label: Equipment Needed
chapters.tag: The full kit list — print this and pack against it
chapters.overview: Everything required to run an EM38 + Dual EMS survey. Treat the checklist as a pre-departure pack list: don't leave the depot until every box is ticked.
chapters.at_a_glance: Missing one cable can cost a whole day of travel. Pack against the checklist, not from memory.

GNSS / GPS receiver + antenna [CONFIRM: model]
Calibration gear [CONFIRM: nulling stand / spacers]

chapters.label: Equipment Setup (Polaris)
chapters.tag: Mounting & wiring the rig on the Polaris
chapters.overview: How to mount, wire and balance the EM38 + Dual EMS on the Polaris for clean data and a safe tow. Follow the order — mechanical mount first, then power, then data, then a function check.
chapters.at_a_glance: The Polaris is for [CONFIRM: terrain / paddock conditions where it's preferred]. Keep the sensor clear of metal and engine interference.

blocks.title: SAFETY FIRST
blocks.text: Engine off and park brake on before mounting anything. Watch pinch points on the tow frame. Confirm the load is secure before moving off — a sensor coming loose at speed is a serious hazard.

items: [CONFIRM: where the mount attaches to the Polaris]
[CONFIRM: how the sensor sled/frame fixes to the mount]
[CONFIRM: required height / ground clearance of the sensor]
Confirm clearance from metal and the engine to avoid interference.

blocks.caption: Photo: EM38 mounted on the Polaris, correct height shown. [ADD PHOTO]

items: [CONFIRM: power source — own battery vs vehicle]
[CONFIRM: connector order and polarity]
Verify the sensor and logger both power up.
[CONFIRM: sensor → logger cable]
[CONFIRM: GNSS → logger cable]
Confirm the logger sees both the sensor and a GPS fix.
Sensor reading and not erroring
GPS fix acquired [CONFIRM: required fix quality]
Logging starts/stops correctly
Load mechanically secure for tow

blocks.title: INTERFERENCE
blocks.text: EM sensors are sensitive to nearby metal and electrical noise. If readings look wrong, re-check the sensor's distance from the vehicle frame before anything else.

chapters.label: Equipment Setup (Ute)
chapters.tag: Mounting & wiring the rig on the Ute
chapters.overview: How to mount, wire and balance the EM38 + Dual EMS for towing behind the Ute. Same logic as the Polaris — mechanical, power, data, check — but the mount and clearances differ.
chapters.at_a_glance: The Ute is for [CONFIRM: larger paddocks / road travel between sites]. Mind the longer tow length on turns and gates.

blocks.title: SAFETY FIRST
blocks.text: Confirm the tow hitch and any safety chains are rated and secured before moving. Check the sensor sled tracks correctly behind the Ute and won't fishtail or strike obstacles.

items: [CONFIRM: hitch type and attachment]
[CONFIRM: sled/cart coupling and safety chains]
[CONFIRM: sensor height / ground clearance]
Confirm tracking and clearance at full turn.

chapters.label: Survey Setup (Electronic)
chapters.tag: Software, logging config, calibration & line plan
chapters.overview: Configuring the logging software and sensor for the job: project setup, calibration/nulling, line spacing and the in-field logging routine. This is where data quality is won or lost.
chapters.at_a_glance: Garbage in stays garbage — calibrate properly and keep line spacing/speed consistent.

items: Open [CONFIRM: logging software name]
[CONFIRM: create new job, naming convention]
[CONFIRM: load paddock boundary / guidance lines]
[CONFIRM: set coordinate system / datum]

blocks.title: NAMING CONVENTION
blocks.text: Use the standard job naming format [CONFIRM exact format] every time. Consistent names are what make exporting and back-office processing painless later.

items: [CONFIRM: calibration procedure for EM38]
[CONFIRM: calibration for Dual EMS]
[CONFIRM: how to verify calibration passed]

blocks.caption: Photo/screenshot: calibration screen with a good result. [ADD]

items: Confirm GPS fix and live sensor reading before the first line.
Start logging at the line start; keep speed steady.
Maintain line spacing using guidance.
Watch the live data for dropouts or spikes.
Stop logging cleanly at the end of each line / the job.

blocks.title: WATCH THE LIVE DATA
blocks.text: Glance at the live trace regularly. Catching a dropout or a flat-lining sensor in the paddock costs minutes; catching it back at the office costs a return trip.

chapters.label: Exporting
chapters.tag: Getting clean data off the device and to the office
chapters.overview: How to close out the job, export the data in the correct format, and get it to the back office reliably. Do this before you leave the paddock where possible.
chapters.at_a_glance: Export and verify on-site. A file you can't open is only a problem if you find out tomorrow.

items: Stop logging and save / close the job in [CONFIRM software].
[CONFIRM: any end-of-job checks the software prompts]
[CONFIRM: export menu / steps]
[CONFIRM: required file format — e.g. CSV / proprietary]
[CONFIRM: file naming to match the job convention]
File opens and has the expected number of points
GPS coordinates present and sensible
No obvious gaps across the paddock
Filename matches the convention
[CONFIRM: upload method — e.g. SharePoint / cloud / sync]
[CONFIRM: where the file goes / which folder]
[CONFIRM: who to notify that the job is in]

blocks.title: DON'T WIPE TOO EARLY
blocks.text: Keep the raw data on the device until you've confirmed it's safely received at the office. [CONFIRM your retention rule.]

chapters.label: Troubleshooting
chapters.overview: Fast field fixes for the most common problems. Find the symptom, work the likely causes top to bottom. If none resolve it, go to Support.
chapters.at_a_glance: Work the table top-down — causes are ordered most-likely first.

rows: Check battery charge, reseat power connectors, try spare battery
Clear sky view, check antenna cable, [CONFIRM correction source]
Reseat data cable, power-cycle sensor, recalibrate
Increase distance from metal, check mount is tight
Confirm logging is active, check free storage
Close job first, re-check export format/steps

blocks.title: BEFORE YOU CALL
blocks.text: Note the exact error message, what you were doing when it happened, and which steps from this table you've already tried. It makes support five times faster.

items: Stop logging and save what you have.
Power-cycle the logger, then the sensor.
Re-confirm power, data and GPS connections.
Recalibrate and run a short test line.
If still faulty, stop and contact Support.

chapters.label: Support
chapters.tag: Who to call, escalation, and reporting issues
chapters.overview: Where to get help when the field fixes run out, and how to feed problems and improvements back so this manual keeps getting better.
chapters.at_a_glance: Stuck after Troubleshooting? Don't burn the day guessing — escalate.

items: Work the Troubleshooting table.
Call field/operational support with your notes.
If unresolved, escalate to technical support.
Log the issue [CONFIRM: where, e.g. ClickUp / form].
